content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of length MMM, we define two functions:


Let f(B)f(B)f(B) denote its bitwise XOR.

That is, f(B)=B1‚äïB2‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïBMf(B) = B_1\oplus B_2\oplus\ldots\oplus B_Mf(B)=B1‚Äã‚äïB2‚Äã‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïBM‚Äã, where ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Let g(B)g(B)g(B) denote the sum of bitwise XORs of all its subarrays.

That is,
g(B)=‚àëi=1M‚àëj=iMf([Bi,Bi+1,‚Ä¶,Bj])g(B) = \sum_{i=1}^M \sum_{j=i}^M f([B_i, B_{i+1}, \ldots, B_j])g(B)=i=1‚àëM‚Äãj=i‚àëM‚Äãf([Bi‚Äã,Bi+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bj‚Äã])Leo had an array AAA containing NNN non-negative integers.

Unfortunately, he forgot the exact values of this array because of an overload of work.

However, he does remember that each element was present in some range: specifically, for each index iii, he knows integers LiL_iLi‚Äã and RiR_iRi‚Äã such that Li‚â§Ai‚â§RiL_i \leq A_i \leq R_iLi‚Äã‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§Ri‚Äã was true.
constraints: The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of Leo's array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers L1,L2,‚Ä¶,LNL_1, L_2, \ldots, L_NL1‚Äã,L2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,LN‚Äã ‚Äî the lower bounds of the array values.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RNR_1, R_2, \ldots, R_NR1‚Äã,R2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äã ‚Äî the upper bounds of the array values.
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of subarray XORs across all possible arrays AAA, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 100010001000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:
There are 444 possible arrays: [3,5,7],[4,5,7],[3,6,7],[4,6,7][3, 5, 7], [4, 5, 7], [3, 6, 7], [4, 6, 7][3,5,7],[4,5,7],[3,6,7],[4,6,7].
Looking at all the subarrays of [3,5,7][3, 5, 7][3,5,7]:

[3][3][3] has a XOR of 333.
[5][5][5] has a XOR of 555.
[7][7][7] has a XOR of 777.
[3,5][3, 5][3,5] has a XOR of 3‚äï5=63\oplus 5 = 63‚äï5=6.
[5,7][5, 7][5,7] has a XOR of 5‚äï7=25\oplus 7 = 25‚äï7=2.
[3,5,7][3, 5, 7][3,5,7] has a XOR of 3‚äï5‚äï7=13\oplus 5\oplus 7 = 13‚äï5‚äï7=1.

They sum up to 242424.
It can be verified that the other arrays have sum of subarray XORs as 25,24,2525, 24, 2525,24,25, for a total sum of 989898.
Test case 222:
The only possible array is [343,10,7,1][343, 10, 7, 1][343,10,7,1].
It can be verified that the sum of its subarray XORs is 143414341434.
